landmark_cases.holding: Federal courts must gate expert testimony for scientific validity (Daubert standard)
landmark_cases.implication: All forensic methodology must satisfy: testability, peer review, error rate, general acceptance

landmark_cases.holding: Daubert applies to all expert testimony, not just scientific
landmark_cases.implication: Forensic experts must satisfy Daubert regardless of discipline

landmark_cases.holding: Expert testimony must be based on generally accepted scientific method
landmark_cases.implication: Still controlling in some states (CA, NY, FL, WA, IL); Daubert controls in federal courts

landmark_cases.holding: DNA swab at arrest is reasonable search (identification purpose)
landmark_cases.implication: Arrestee DNA collection without warrant upheld

landmark_cases.holding: No constitutional right to post-conviction DNA testing
landmark_cases.implication: Access to biological evidence post-conviction governed by state law

landmark_cases.holding: Fingerprint comparison testimony limited after Daubert challenge (later reversed)
landmark_cases.implication: ACE-V methodology must be documented; examiner must explain error rate
